name: MT Gertrudes
classification_notes: Designed as a geometric sans-serif explored across three ideological variants: a traditional geometric approach (A), a hybrid (B), and a more angular, dogmatic interpretation (C).
summary: A geometric sans-serif in three ideologically distinct sub-families, ranging from traditional circular forms to angular, rule-driven construction.
description: Designed by Simon Bent and released in 2024, MT Gertrudes splits across three sub-families sharing common metrics and weights but arriving at markedly different results. Gertrudes A follows conventional geometric sans principles; Gertrudes B occupies a middle ground; Gertrudes C pushes toward harder, more angular letterforms. All three span ten weights from Thin to Heavy, with broad Latin Extended coverage and a full complement of OpenType features.
foundry_blurb: Modern Type describe Gertrudes B as the combination of two design ideologies, with A representing a traditional geometric sans approach and C a more dogmatic stance favouring angular character forms. All three sub-families share common metrics, weights and some characters, yet produce three distinctly different feeling typefaces.

name: MT Everyday Sans
classification_notes: A low-contrast grotesque with straight terminals and a heavier-than-normal axis, placing it firmly in the contemporary grotesque tradition with an upright, neutral construction.
summary: A ten-weight grotesque by Dalton Bruyns built for general-purpose use, moving from neutral lighter weights to a more characterful heaviness at the top of the range.
description: Designed by Dalton Bruyns and released in 2023, the family spans Extra Light through Heavy across ten upright weights with no italic cut. Straight terminals and low contrast keep the lighter weights neutral and readable, while the heavier-than-normal axis lends the bolder cuts a playful quality without sacrificing coherence across the range. Broad Latin character coverage with Cyrillic and Greek support, oldstyle figures and discretionary ligatures round out the feature set.
foundry_blurb: Modern Type describe MT Everyday Sans as a low-contrast grotesque with straight terminals that offers a contemporary feel within a classically constructed framework. Designed by Dalton Bruyns, it is intended as an all-round, easy-to-use typeface suited to everyday tasks, with lighter weights remaining neutral and heavier weights becoming more playful in character.

name: MT Mighty Mono
classification_notes: A monospaced typeface with geometric construction and low contrast, explicitly described as geometric and monospaced by the foundry, with letterforms drawn from the designer's Vietnamese schooling background.
summary: A low-contrast geometric monospaced typeface by Thy Hà, rooted in Vietnamese schoolbook letterforms and developed through postgraduate research into Vietnamese diacritics.
description: Drawn by Thy Hà during her master's research project on Vietnamese diacritic design, the typeface takes its letterforms from those she encountered in primary school in Vietnam, including a tailed 'i' and single-storey 'a' and 'g'. Five weights run from Light to Bold without italics, and the glyph set covers extensive Latin Extended characters with full Vietnamese support. Its geometric rigour and monospaced rhythm make it suited to editorial, coding and display contexts where a non-generic mono is required.
foundry_blurb: Modern Type describe MT Mighty Mono as a low-contrast, geometric monospaced typeface designed by Thy Hà, with letterforms drawn from her Vietnamese primary school education. The typeface originated as part of her master's research project titled 'Up? Down? Left? Right?', which focused on designing Vietnamese diacritics. Production was handled by Dalton Bruyns, and the typeface was released in 2023.

name: MT Mizzen
classification_notes: The page does not describe the typeface in detail, but the name, weight range and italic pairings suggest a contemporary humanist sans-serif drawn for text and editorial use.
summary: A seven-weight humanist sans-serif with matching italics, designed by Simon Bent and released by Modern Type in 2025.
description: Spanning Light through Heavy with italic companions at each weight, MT Mizzen offers a broad tonal range for text and editorial settings. Designed by Simon Bent with production by Dalton Bruyns, the family is currently at version 0.3, suggesting an active development cycle. The weight range and italic coverage make it a practical choice for publications and UI work requiring typographic flexibility.
foundry_blurb: MT Mizzen is a typeface designed by Simon Bent with production by Dalton Bruyns, released by Modern Type in 2025. The family comprises fourteen styles across seven weights from Light to Heavy, each with a matching italic.

name: MT Update Sans
classification_notes: Described as a flared grotesque drawing on 1970s–80s type manuals, with mono-like proportions — placing it firmly in the grotesque tradition with a humanist inflection from the flared terminals.
summary: A flared grotesque drawn from 1970s and 80s type manuals, built with mono-like proportions and a wide weight range from Hairline to Heavy.
description: Designed by Ana Andreeva and Dalton Bruyns and released in 2024, MT Update Sans takes cues from mid-century manual typography to produce a proportional grotesque with flared terminals. The nine-weight range stretches from an extreme Hairline through to Heavy, giving the family reach across tight editorial settings and heavier display use. Its uniform proportions, borrowed in spirit from monospaced conventions, are intended to support expansion across different scripts and spacing systems.
foundry_blurb: Modern Type describe Update Sans as a flared grotesque inspired by type manuals from the 70s and 80s, using these as a starting point to build a mono-like, proportional font. The family's stylistic flexibility is intended to extend across scripts and spacing conventions, with laser-thin weights designed to fit the tightest of settings.

name: MT Archi
designer: Ana Andreeva, Dalton Bruyns, Katie Fridman
classification_notes: The typeface presents as a grotesque sans-serif with a wide weight range; its extra-low cap height and extender lengths suggest a practical, architecturally influenced grotesque designed for dense text settings.
summary: A grotesque sans with an extra-low cap height engineered for tight line spacing and cap-heavy settings such as German-language architectural publications.
description: Designed by Ana Andreeva, Dalton Bruyns and Katie Fridman and released in 2024, MT Archi reduces cap height and extender lengths to allow for denser vertical rhythms without sacrificing legibility. Eleven weights from Super Hairline to Heavy each carry a matching italic, giving the family considerable range for publication work. The stated target is architectural publishing, where compact, structured setting is the norm.
foundry_blurb: Modern Type describe Archi as a typeface with an extra-low cap height and shortened extender lengths, designed specifically to accommodate tight line spacing and cap-heavy languages such as German, making it a practical choice for architectural publications.

name: MT Callisto
classification_notes: The name and single-style release offer limited classification signals, but the specimen text and foundry context suggest a traditional serif design; classified as transitional serif pending further evidence.
summary: A single-weight serif released in 2025 by Simon Bent and Dalton Bruyns under the Modern Type foundry.
description: MT Callisto is a one-style serif at version 0.1, suggesting an early or deliberate minimal release. Designed by Simon Bent and Dalton Bruyns, with production credited to Dalton Bruyns, it offers a single Regular weight with no italic or condensed companions at this stage. The limited specimen and early version number indicate the typeface may see further development.
foundry_blurb: Modern Type lists MT Callisto as a 2025 release at version 0.1, designed by Simon Bent and Dalton Bruyns, with production by Dalton Bruyns. It is currently available in a single Regular style.

name: Astral Grotesk
classification_notes: Explicitly described as a display grotesk drawing on 1970s typography; the single style and character-forward approach place it firmly in the display category rather than a text grotesque.
summary: A character-forward display grotesque from Simon Bent, drawing on the expressive typography of the 1970s rather than pursuing neutrality.
description: Designed by Simon Bent and due for full release in late 2025, this display grotesque wears its personality openly, rooted in the dynamic and often idiosyncratic type culture of the 1970s. Five weights run from Light to Bold within a single upright style, reflecting the early-stage nature of the release at version 0.3. It positions itself against the prevailing tendency toward neutral grotesques, favouring a more distinctive voice.
foundry_blurb: Modern Type describe Astral Grotesk as a testament to the enduring charm of classic display grotesks, drawing inspiration from the dynamic typography of the 1970s to produce a typeface that embraces its own distinctive personality rather than striving for neutrality.

name: MT Beak Sans
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a neo-grotesque by the foundry, with ink traps as a distinguishing detail. The single regular weight and clean structure confirm a neo-grotesque classification.
summary: A neo-grotesque with beak-shaped ink traps threaded throughout its forms, designed by Ana Andreeva for everyday use.
description: Designed by Ana Andreeva with production by Dalton Bruyns, this neo-grotesque uses beak-like ink traps as a consistent structural motif, adding contrast and visual interest without departing from the genre's neutral character. Released in version 0.1 in 2024, the family currently consists of a single Regular weight, with a companion display family, MT Beak, planned for future release. Early-stage but shows a clear design rationale.
foundry_blurb: Modern Type describe MT Beak Sans as a modern neo-grotesque for everyday use, with beak-like ink traps distributed throughout the letterforms to balance openness, contrast and personality. They note that a related display family, MT Beak, is planned for late 2024.
